calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS ORDER OF EXAMINATION CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Off Calendar

SMALL CLAIMS ORDER OF EXAMINATION CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-05-2015 Continued For Letter To Feb-26-15 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 506

SMALL CLAIMS ORDER OF EXAMINATION CALENDAR
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Continued To Feb-05-15 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 506

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Set For Trial On Jul-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 318

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-10-2014 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on Wednesday, July 9, 2014 in Department 318:
Arlene Brown, Plaintiff / Respondent, appears in Propria Persona with witness Niya Brown;
Natalya Belousova, Defendant / Appellant, appears in Propria Persona with Russian translator / witness Edmond Amcayn and witness Jennifer Willis.

This causes comes on calendar this day, as assigned by Department 206, for trial de novo.
All parties and witnesses are sworn. The clerk administers the interpreter's oath to
Mr. Amcayn to translate the English language into the Russian language and vice versa. Plaintiff / Respondent pre-marks Exhibits A through E. Defendant / Appellant pre-marks Exhibits 1 through 11.

Arlene Brown testifies on her on behalf. The following documentary evidence are introduced:
Exihibit A - Emails from interested parties from out-of-state
Exhibit E - Packet sent to Ms. Belousova (sealed envelope opened in court), including photographs, receipts
Exhibit F - Lease
Exhibit B - Documents containing handwritten lease, list of problems

Natalya Belousova, through Edmond Amcayn Russian interpreter, cross examines Arlene Brown.

Niya Brown testifies on behalf of Arlene Brown.

Natalya Belousova testifies on her on behalf through Russian interpreter Edmond Amcayn. The following documentary evidence are introduced:
Exhibit 1 - Contract to Section 8
Exhibit 2 - Pictures, receipts
Exhibit 4 - Section 8 do not pass list, inspection list
Exhibit 6 - 30-Day Notice
Exhibit 3 - Pictures of moving out condition

Jennifer Willis testifies on behalf of Natalya Belousova. The following documentary evidence are introduced:
Exhibit 11 - Email from Jennifer Willis to Tracy Burton dated April 9, 2013
Exhibit 10 - Recitation of meeting

The Court hears further argument by Niya Brown and Arlene Brown.
The Court hears further argument by Natalya Belousova.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the Court takes this matter under submission.

Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar May-27-2014 Judgment Entered
